perturbation.original_text: 3. All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. All Confidential Information delivered pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party, and any documents containing or reflecting the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option, unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties. Nothing herein shall be construed as granting or conferr ing any rights by license or otherwise, express or implied, regarding any idea made, conceived or acquired prior to or after the Effective Date, nor as granting any right with respect to the use or marketing of any product or service. The Parties shall us e the Confidential Information only for the Business Relationship. 2 The obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue and survive the completion or abandonment of the Business Relationship and shall remain binding for a period of two (2) y ears from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties" to the requirement to return or destroy confidential information, the obligation to do so is no longer absolute. This contradicts the previous sections which emphasize the importance of maintaining confidentiality and returning all information. It creates a loophole that could allow a party to retain and potentially use confidential information if a separate agreement is made, which dilutes the purpose of the NDA. This creates an in-text contradiction because the previous sections make the NDA all about maintaining confidentiality, but this change allows for an opt-out clause that is not clearly defined or limited, thereby going against the strict enforcement expected from the original context.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Parties shall (i) use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the confidentiality of the information and materials, whether oral, written or in any form whatsoever, of the other that may be reasonably understood, from legend s, the nature of such information itself and/or the circumstances of such information’s disclosure, to be confidential and/or proprietary thereto or to third parties to which either of them owes a duty of nondisclosure (collectively, “Confidential Informat ion”); (ii) take commercially reasonable action in connection therewith, including without limitation at least the action that each takes to protect the confidentiality of its comparable proprietary assets; (iii) to the extent within their respective possession and/or control, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, immediately return to the provider thereof all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used or enjoyed after termination or expiration hereof, and (iv) with respect to any perso n to which disclosure is contemplated, require such person to execute an agreement providing for the treatment of Confidential Information set forth in clauses (i) through (iii).
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3 stated that confidential information "shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party upon written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Party’s option." The changed text adds "unless otherwise agreed in writing by both Parties," creating an exception to the mandatory return or destruction. This directly contradicts Section 1 which requires the parties to maintain confidentiality and return all Confidential Information not licensed or authorized to be used, meaning they can agree to circumvent that obligation now, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there is no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will be entitled to recover from the other Party all costs incurred thereby, including without limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there may be no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will only be entitled to recover from the other Party costs incurred thereby to the extent that such costs are proportionate to the damages suffered, including with limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "there is no adequate remedy at law" to "there may be no adequate remedy at law" introduces uncertainty about the availability of legal remedies. This undermines the justification for equitable relief (injunction) and makes it harder to predict the outcome of a breach. Additionally, limiting the recovery of costs to those 'proportionate to the damages suffered' contradicts the principle of full indemnification for breach, potentially discouraging enforcement. This creates an in-text contradiction with the aim of the agreement, which is to protect confidential information; limiting costs undermines the willingness of either party to defend the agreement in court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. As a violation by either Party of this Agreement could cause irreparable injury to the other Party and as there may be no adequate remedy at law for such violation, the non -breaching Party may, in additi on to any other remedies available to it at law or in equity, enjoin the breaching Party in a court of equity for violating or threatening to violate this Agreement. In the event either Party is required to enforce this Agreement through legal action, the n it will only be entitled to recover from the other Party costs incurred thereby to the extent that such costs are proportionate to the damages suffered, including with limitation, reasonable attorney’s fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the condition regarding legal remedies from 'there is no adequate remedy at law' to 'there may be no adequate remedy at law', introducing uncertainty. Additionally, it limits the recovery of costs to those 'proportionate to the damages suffered,' deviating from the original text's intention. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed text, a clear contradiction is introduced. The original text indicated full indemnification, but the changed text limits costs, creating a direct conflict regarding the extent of compensation available and creating ambiguity. This creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of . Neither Party shall be deemed to waive any of its rights, powers or remedies hereunder unless such waiver is in writing and signed by said Party. This Agreement is binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their successor and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of . Neither Party shall be deemed to waive any of its rights, powers or remedies hereunder unless such waiver is in writing and signed by said Party, except for implied waivers resulting from a course of dealing. This Agreement is binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their successor and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "except for implied waivers resulting from a course of dealing," the contract now allows for a party to implicitly waive its rights through its actions, even if there isn't a written waiver. This creates conflict with the original statement, which required all waivers to be written and signed. This undermines the protection given to the Parties, and creates ambiguity about whether they can imply something in between them, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This Agreement is to be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the state of . Neither Party shall be deemed to waive any of its rights, powers or remedies hereunder unless such waiver is in writing and signed by said Party, except for implied waivers resulting from a course of dealing. This Agreement is binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their successor and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing for 'implied waivers resulting from a course of dealing' to the original clause stating that no rights, powers, or remedies are waived unless the waiver is in writing and signed. This directly contradicts the original requirement for written and signed waivers, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the document. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, indicating that the change itself introduces the contradiction. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this undermines the protection given to the Parties, creating ambiguity and a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement constitu tes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and is intended as the Parties’ final expression and complete and exclusive statement of the terms thereof, superseding all prior or contemporaneous agreem ents, representations, promises and understandings, whether written or oral. Neither Party is to be bound by any pre -printed terms appearing in the other Party’s form documents, tariffs, purchase orders, quotations, acknowledgments, invoices, or other ins truments. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement constitu tes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and is intended as the Parties’ final expression and complete and exclusive statement of the terms thereof, superseding all prior or contemporaneous agreem ents, representations, promises and understandings, whether written or oral. Neither Party is to be bound by any pre -printed terms appearing in the other Party’s form documents, tariffs, purchase orders, quotations, acknowledgments, invoices, or other ins truments. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties, unless such modification is implied through the parties conduct.
perturbation.explanation: The final sentence originally stated the agreement can only be modified with a written document signed by both parties. Adding "unless such modification is implied through the parties conduct" introduces a contradiction. Now, a party could argue the agreement was modified through behavior, regardless of whether a written amendment exists, directly contradicting the written amendment requirement. This creates an in-text contradiction which can cause confusion during enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. This Agreement constitu tes the entire agreement and understanding of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and is intended as the Parties’ final expression and complete and exclusive statement of the terms thereof, superseding all prior or contemporaneous agreem ents, representations, promises and understandings, whether written or oral. Neither Party is to be bound by any pre -printed terms appearing in the other Party’s form documents, tariffs, purchase orders, quotations, acknowledgments, invoices, or other ins truments. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by an instrument in writing signed by both Parties, unless such modification is implied through the parties conduct.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement can only be modified by a written instrument signed by both parties. The changed text adds "unless such modification is implied through the parties conduct." This creates a direct contradiction because it introduces an alternative method of modification (conduct) that bypasses the written requirement, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation and falls under the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
